response.justification: Strong technical alignment with the role's developer tools and infrastructure focus—the candidate has built CI/CD systems, test runners, code generators, and currently works on developer tooling at Google. However, there is a critical gap: zero formal people management experience despite the role requiring a minimum of 2 years. The extensive teaching and mentoring background (workshops, open source leadership) demonstrates leadership potential, but is not equivalent to managing teams with performance reviews and organizational accountability. This candidate would be better suited to a principal engineer or technical staff track unless willing to take a management role with external support.
strengths: 20+ years of software development with strong current track record (Staff Engineer at Google, recent Senior roles at Meta)
Direct hands-on experience building developer tools, testing infrastructure (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts library), and CI/CD systems (Altom GitLab/Docker setup)
Demonstrated ability to teach and mentor through workshops, code katas, open source projects, and community courses
concerns: No formal people management experience mentioned anywhere on resume—a minimum 2-year requirement is completely absent
No evidence of leading organizational change or managing teams through hiring, performance reviews, or cross-functional alignment
Mobile platform experience is limited (one iOS/AppleTV role years ago); most recent work is backend/full-stack, not mobile ecosystem focused

key_factors.factor: Expert-level developer tools and infrastructure background
